title: Feed Discovery, URL Identity, Scope, and SSRF Boundary · Program Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss Go decisions for feed discovery, url identity, scope, and ssrf boundary and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, compiler, network, XML, PostgreSQL, race, load, accessibility, deployment, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for authorized feed discovery and network-scope policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practi…
objectives: Distinguish a direct feed URL from HTML autodiscovery and require an explicit bounded discovery policy.
Admit only alternate links with supported RSS or Atom media types, resolve references against the response URL, and bound candidates.
Normalize only justified URI components while preserving distinctions that can identify different publisher resources.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.instruction:

Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. options.text: Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim does not survive the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary.

For diagnose the hidden failure, import one convenient trusted RSS feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, XML and HTTP budgets, PostgreSQL constraints and transaction, schedule, lease, goroutine cleanup, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as feed-service proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Feed Go Discovery Provenance
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Feed Go Discovery Provenance: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go team handles case 8347 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile bounded feed discovery, URL identity, SSRF decisions, and provenance; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Retain submitted URL, response URL, selected candidate, redirect chain, resolution evidence, and operator decision.
